elevator_pitch: The V.I.B. (Very Important Bathroom) is a subscription-based network of premium portable restrooms. For $9.99/month, badge into clean, sanitized, music-playing restrooms placed throughout your city—at office parks, events, downtown areas, and parks. It's the private members' club your bladder has been waiting for.
problem: Portable restrooms are universally despised—dirty, smelly, and degrading. Yet the $7B industry hasn't innovated in decades. Office workers in gender-imbalanced workplaces wait in endless lines, concertgoers brave horrifying honey buckets, and park visitors just hold it. Everyone deserves better.
solution: The V.I.B. deploys a citywide network of premium portable restrooms accessible only to subscribers. Each unit is climate-controlled, regularly sanitized, and plays ambient music. Badge-in access limits traffic and creates accountability, keeping every unit clean. An app shows real-time availability and locations near you.
target_market: Urban professionals in office parks with inadequate facilities, frequent event attendees, outdoor enthusiasts, and gig economy workers (delivery drivers, rideshare) who need reliable restroom access. Secondary: event organizers wanting a premium restroom option for their venues.
revenue_model: Monthly subscriptions at $9.99/month per individual. Corporate plans for office parks at $500-2,000/month. Event rental packages at premium rates. Per-use swipe option at $1-2 for non-subscribers. Hardware revenue from selling/leasing premium units to venue operators.
key_features: Badge-in access with real-time availability on the app
Climate-controlled, sanitized, and ambient music in every unit
Citywide network coverage at offices, parks, downtown, and events
Accountability system—badge tracking keeps every unit pristine
competitive_advantage: The portable restroom industry has had zero consumer-facing innovation. Honey Bucket and competitors compete on price for a commodity product. The V.I.B. creates an entirely new premium tier with recurring revenue, brand loyalty, and network effects. Badge access solves the vandalism and overcrowding problems that make current porta-potties terrible.
market_size_estimate: The global portable restroom market is $7B+ with steady year-over-year growth. Premium restroom rentals for events already command 3-5x standard pricing. A network subscription model across 10 major U.S. cities with 50,000 subscribers each = $60M+ ARR before corporate and event revenue.
go_to_market: Start with 20-30 units in one city, placed at high-traffic office parks and near popular outdoor venues. Offer corporate partnerships first (guaranteed revenue). Build consumer base through events and word-of-mouth. Expand city-by-city once unit economics are proven.
risks: High upfront capital required to manufacture and place enough units for network density
Vandalism and misuse despite badge system could erode the premium experience
Consumer willingness to pay monthly for restroom access is unproven in the U.S. market
year_one_milestones: Deploy 30 premium units across one metro area with 2,000+ active subscribers
Sign 5 corporate office park partnerships for guaranteed recurring revenue
Prove unit economics: each unit generating 2x its monthly maintenance cost
startup_cost_range: $150,000 - $400,000 (premium unit manufacturing, placement logistics, app development, initial maintenance operations)
